# UC-20.1.18 · Orphaned Cloud Resource Detection

## Description

Unattached volumes, unused elastic IPs, old snapshots without volume — **inventory-driven** waste beyond idle CPU (UC-20.1.4).

## Value

Unattached volumes, unused elastic IPs, old snapshots without volume — **inventory-driven** waste beyond idle CPU (UC-20.1.4).

## Implementation

Refresh Config aggregator daily. Include unattached EIP and old snapshots in companion searches. Route to owners via account tags.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: AWS Config snapshot, resource inventory.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `aws:config:inventory`, cost.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Refresh Config aggregator daily. Include unattached EIP and old snapshots in companion searches. Route to owners via account tags.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud_inventory sourcetype="aws:config:inventory" earliest=-1d
| where status="available" AND resource_type="AWS::EC2::Volume" AND attachments=0
| lookup monthly_storage_rate region OUTPUT rate_gb_mo
| eval waste=size_gb*rate_gb_mo
| stats sum(waste) as monthly_waste by account_id, region
| sort -monthly_waste
```

Understanding this SPL

**Orphaned Cloud Resource Detection** — Unattached volumes, unused elastic IPs, old snapshots without volume — **inventory-driven** waste beyond idle CPU (UC-20.1.4).

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:config:inventory`, cost. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): AWS Config snapshot, resource inventory. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud_inventory; **sourcetype**: aws:config:inventory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud_inventory, sourcetype="aws:config:inventory", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where status="available" AND resource_type="AWS::EC2::Volume" AND attachments=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **waste** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by account_id, region** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (orphan waste $), Bar chart (by account), Single value (total orphan monthly $).

## SPL

```spl
index=cloud_inventory sourcetype="aws:config:inventory" earliest=-1d
| where status="available" AND resource_type="AWS::EC2::Volume" AND attachments=0
| lookup monthly_storage_rate region OUTPUT rate_gb_mo
| eval waste=size_gb*rate_gb_mo
| stats sum(waste) as monthly_waste by account_id, region
| sort -monthly_waste
```

## Visualization

Table (orphan waste $), Bar chart (by account), Single value (total orphan monthly $).
